observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. exert efforts in the identification of the nature of the unrecorded deposits and
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for their proper recording in the books;
b. locate and transmit the disbursement vouchers, together with the supporting
documents, of the identified unrecorded disbursements to the Municipal
Accountant for recording in the books; and
c. make representations with the bank to obtain copies of the credit and debit memos;
prepare the necessary supporting documents and forward them to the Municipal
Accountant for recording in the books.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant make diligent
efforts to determine the concerned employees and the corresponding amount involved
in the under and over remittances of salary loan; and subsequently, demand collection
of under-remitted amounts from those liable and arrange for the reimbursement of
excess remittances to the concerned employees.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review, identify and investigate the
unreconciled difference of ₱1,363,243.64 in the General Fund Proper as reflected in
the BRS and effect any correction/adjustment, provided they are valid transactions
supported by complete documentation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the
cashbook balances with the SLs of the Municipal Accountant and identify as soon as
possible the discrepancies between their records so that necessary adjustments can be
reflected in the respective books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. issue a memorandum directing all accountable officers, who are still employed in
the municipality, to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances, otherwise,
cause the suspension/withholding of their salaries;
b. send demand letters to accountable officers who are no longer working with the
municipality to compel them to settle their outstanding cash advances; and
c. stop the granting of cash advances for special activities to personnel who are
neither duly bonded nor designated as disbursing officers.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the supplies and materials to the
inventory account upon purchase and recognize expense only upon issuance thereof
pursuant to the asset method of accounting for recording purchases of supplies and
materials as prescribed under Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
journal entries to reclassify the PPE items with costs below P50,000.00
capitalization threshold, to the appropriate semi-expendable
property/expense/Prior Period Adjustment accounts, for fair and accurate
presentation of the affected account balances in the Financial Statements.
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Inventory Committee together with the GSO to devise a strategy to expedite
the completion of the physical count of all municipal’s property, whether
acquired through purchase or donation, including those constructed by
administration and found at station and prepare the RPCPPE, copy furnish
the Audit Team;
b. GSO and Municipal Accountant to perform reconciliation of PPE balances
per subsidiary records, property cards and the result of physical count
contained in the RPCPPE and investigate the discrepancies between their
records; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) investigate and exert extra effort to determine the
details of “Prior Year,” “Prior Year 2002 @ GL,” or “Prior Year 2003 @ GL”
PPE items for proper reconciliation with the records of GSO and adjustment
in the accounting books; and if found futile, avail of the sunset provision of
One-Time Cleansing; and (ii) review the PPE accounts with abnormal or
negative balances due to the recognition of accumulated depreciation with no
acquisition cost, and make the necessary adjustments to correct the errors and
present the PPE accounts to their proper carrying amounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant, (i) prepare the necessary journal entries to transfer the cost
of completed projects to the appropriate PPE accounts and recognize the
corresponding depreciation; and (ii) develop a strategy or tool for the proper
monitoring of projects, ensuring that project costs are accurately tracked and that
completed projects are promptly and correctly recorded in the PPE accounts; and
b. Municipal Budget Officer maintain a separate RAAO for each specific project
instead of aggregating projects into a single group, in order to facilitate easy
monitoring and tracking of appropriations, allotments, and obligations on a
per-project basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute the Disposal
Committee to initiate the proper disposal of all obsolete and unserviceable
properties, in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445 and the procedures
outlined in the Revised Manual on the Disposal of Government Properties.
We also recommended that the GSO conduct an inventory of the previously
returned unserviceable properties and thereafter initiate the request for their
disposal to help prevent storage congestion and further deterioration of the items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to conduct a thorough reconciliation of the records for the Due to
GSIS, Due to Pag-IBIG, and Due to PhilHealth accounts, specifically addressing
the under-remittance of ₱1,381,081.88, and the abnormal balance of ₱219,346.08
in the subsidiary ledgers, and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to ensure
the prompt and accurate remittance of said amount to the concerned government
agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan to revisit
and update the existing ordinance on the daily rate of JO personnel to ensure
alignment with the prevailing minimum wage rate.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council exercise prudence in the formulation of the development plans and public investment programs to ensure that the 20% DF is used solely for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

assess the cost of acquiring uniform size tables to be used during Market Day and provide an appropriation for that purpose; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor:

a. thru the Municipal Accountant, (i) send demand letters to the accountable officers, who are still connected with the Municipality including Ms. Sapalo, and require the immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances, otherwise, cause the suspension/withholding of their salaries and any amount due to them; and

b. submit the necessary papers/documents required by COA Circular No. 2016-005 and in our letter-reply dated February 17, 2021 to facilitate the evaluation of request for authority from COA to write-off the dormant accounts pertaining to the ₱11,090,292.07 cash advances of the deceased accountable officers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor (a) designate a Focal Person who shall coordinate closely with the concerned Implementing Agencies and demand for the immediate submission of Liquidation Reports/Fund Utilization Reports/Report of Disbursements for recording of completed projects in the books of accounts, as well as refund of unused balances, if any; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to determine the unreconciled amount of ₱455,058.81.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer conduct inspection and assessment on the actual accomplishments/status of implementation of the projects to serve as basis of the Focal Person in demanding the immediate completion of the projects and/or liquidation of the fund transfers.

We reiterated our recommendation that the:

a. Inventory Committee complete the conduct of physical count of all PPE of the Municipality and prepare the inventory report thereon and furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE together with its summary showing the PPE classification; and

b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant thereafter reconcile the Report on the Physical Count of PPE with Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, and other accounting records, taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor reconstitute the Inventory Committee with the primary goal of completing the One-Time Cleansing that is well represented from all concerned departments preferably holding a position of next lower rank from the division chief and adhere to Item 5.3 of COA Circular 2020-006.

We also recommended that the Property Inventory Committee submit to the Audit Team the revised PIP at least 10 days before the inventory taking, as well as the requested documents provided in our letter reply dated November 12, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up the necessary accounting entries to adjust the affected account balances for their fair presentation in the financial statements, such as: (a) reversal of the previously capitalized interest expense and penalties; (b) recognition of 100% completed project components from the Construction in Progress account to the appropriate PPE account; and (c) thereafter, allocate the costs of the water supply systems components over its estimated useful life through recognition of depreciation expenses.

We recommended that the Acing Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. ensure that the ₱5,000.00 minimum take home pay of officials and employees is observed in compliance with the provisions of the special provisions of the GAA by advising the concerned officials and employees whose salaries fell below ₱5,000.00 to make direct payment of loan amortizations to the bank or lending institution;

b. refrain from issuing certifications to accommodate payment of loan amortizations to bank and other lending institutions through payroll deduction if it will reduce the officials’ and employees’ monthly net take home pay below the minimum amount; and

c. separate the payroll for RATA from the payment of salaries to ensure proper computation of the net take home pay.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Mayor:

a. instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly review and validate the supporting documents of the employees’ claims for annual mental and medical-physical examination to ensure that these are in accordance with A.O No. 402 and its IRR and that its payment is based on the cost of the actual check-up expenses incurred;

b. discontinue the reimbursement of medicine expenses like prescription drugs, vitamins, maintenance and similar items, as this is not within the purview of A.O. No. 402 and its IRR;

c. discontinue the practice of processing and paying for the hospital bills of the employees as this is a duplication of the Universal Health Care Act; and

d. modify the provisions of Administrative Order no. 01, series of 2021, in accordance with the provisions of A.O. No. 402 and its IRR.

We also recommended that the Municipal Vice Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan members:

a. repeal and/or amend the provisions of Ordinance No. 2019-03 that are inconsistent with the provisions of A.O. No. 402 and its IRR; and

b. conduct extensive research and thoroughly discuss during deliberations taking into consideration the provisions of the national laws, its IRR and any amendments thereto before adopting it in the municipal level to forestall deviation/s therefrom and to ensure that enacted ordinances are solid basis of the Executive Department during budget execution and thus guarantee smooth implementation of ordinances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

thru the Municipal Accountant, (i) send demand letters to all accountable officers who are still connected with the Municipality including Ms. Sapalo, and require immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances, otherwise, cause the suspension/withholding of their salaries and any amount due to them; and (ii) make correcting entries for the erroneous credits to Due from Officers and Employees account totaling ₱55,422.00 incurred during set-up of payables; 

submit the necessary papers/documents embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-005 to facilitate the evaluation of request for authority from COA to write-off dormant accounts pertaining to the ₱11,090,292.07 cash advances of the deceased accountable officers; and 

strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1992 on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant sent the demand letters to the concerned Accountable Officers on June 22, 2021.




The Municipal Accountant made the correcting entries on January 04, 2021.




The Management already submitted their request for write-off including a certification from the HRMO that Mr. Medina already claimed his Terminal Leave Benefits (TLB).




The Management has implemented a “no liquidation, no cash advance policy” to avoid accumulation of outstanding cash advances and undertake that this will not recur in the future.
observations.reason: The Management failed to submit all the documents embodied in COA Circular No, 2016-005. Likewise, the HRMO did not mention the date when Mr. Medina claimed his TLB.

observations.recommendation:

b. Submit the necessary papers/documents embodied in COA Circular No.
2016-005 to facilitate the evaluation of request for authority from COA to
write-off dormant accounts pertaining to the ₱11,090,292.07 cash advances of
the deceased accountable officers; and
c. Strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February

We reiterated our recommendation that the:

and furnish the Audit Team with copy of the Report on the Physical Count of
PPE together with its summary showing the PPE classification for

b. GSO and the Municipal Accountant thereafter reconcile the Report on the

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:

Development Coordinator to revalidate and verify any third party claims on
the road lots. Thereafter, require the Municipal Assessor (i) to submit the

penalty charges for late payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: i. observations.managementAction: The appropriation for equipment, materials and supplies needed to operate the slaughterhouse was already provided in CY 2020. The audit observation was received by the Management after it has finalized the budget for CY 2019; thus the recommendation was budgeted in CY 2020 and implemented in that budgetary year.
observations.reason: The Audit Observation was received by the Management after it has finalized the budget for CY 2019. Thus the recommendation was budgeted in CY 2020 and implemented in that budgetary year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook
balances with the Subsidiary Ledgers of the Municipal Accountant for the Cash
in Bank accounts every end of the month pursuant to Section 63.F. of the LTOM
and Section 181(c) of the GAAM, Volume I, to ensure that all transactions are
correctly reported to the Municipal Accountant and duly recorded in the books
of accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant regularly issue a
certification on the balances of all Accountable Officers that maintain a
cashbook for their reference in the reconciliation of their cashbook balances
with the SLs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. File request for authority from COA to write-off dormant accounts
pertaining to the ₱11,090,292.07 cash advances of the late former Municipal
Administrators and the late former Municipal Treasurer, duly supported
with necessary papers/documents as embodied in COA Circular No. 2016-
005;
b. Send demand letters, thru the Municipal Accountant, to all accountable
officers, who are still connected with the Municipality including Ms. Sapalo,
and require them immediate settlement of their outstanding cash advances,
otherwise, cause the suspension/withholding of their salaries; and
c. Strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February
10, 1992 on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Assessor and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to
revalidate and verify any third party claims on the road lots before the
Municipal Accountant finally recognize the same in the books for better
presentation of the Road Network Account balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant apply the change in
accounting policy on depreciation of PPE to reflect the correct balance of
affected accounts in the Financial Statements and provide adequate disclosure
therefor in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the General Services Officer and the BAC to comply with the
provision of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 concerning
procurement of CSE;
b. Instruct the BAC to consolidate all CSE purchase requests of all
departments as shown in the Annual Procurement Plan and utilize the
functionality of the mPhilGEPS in ordering CSE through the Virtual
Store; and
c. Require the GSO to fully accomplish all relevant entries in the Purchase
Orders to provide useful information to all users of data therein and issue a
BAC Resolution recommending the HoPE to resort into an alternative
mode of procurement and the HoPE’s approval.

We recommended that the (a) requesting department heads stop indicating
brand names of requested goods in the PRs; and (b) Municipal Mayor refrain
from approving PRs with reference to brand names and ensure that all
requesting officers comply strictly with Section 18 of the 2016 Revised IRR of
R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit the distribution list of
the indigent students who received the school supplies indicating therein the date
of distribution for our reference in audit.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:
a. Exercise prudence in all procurement activities of the Municipality by
observing strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184;
b. Stop the practice of splitting requisitions/PRs to circumvent the
requirements of competitive bidding by consolidating requisitions needed at
or almost the same time; and
c. Submit the BAC Resolutions recommending to the Municipal Mayor to
resort to an alternative mode of procurement and the Municipal Mayor’s
approval thereon for our reference in audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor inform all concerned
department heads to review the necessity and regularity of the items requested
for procurement that are indispensable in the performance of their functions
and mandate to prevent incurrence of unnecessary and illegal expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Instruct the MSWDO to (i) strictly abide by the guidelines in the granting of
financial assistance taking into consideration the noted deficiencies in audit;
(ii) establish a database of assistance granted to monitor its frequency; (iii)
thoroughly screen/interview the clients to properly address his/her concerns;
(iv) require complete documentary requirements from the client before the
release of assistance; and (v) refrain from accommodating financial
assistance facilitated by the Legislative personnel and prospectively, advise
all clients to directly seek assistance at their Office.
b. Strengthen free medical services like free consultation, medicines, dental,
laboratory and vaccines by providing the Municipal Health Officer with
complete medical facilities and adequate supplies for better delivery of health
services to the general public;
c. Divert portion of the budget of the MSWDO intended for medical assistance
to the Municipal Health Office for procurement of medicines and vaccines; and
d. Enter into a Memorandum of Agreement with the service providers for
timely rendition of social services to the disadvantaged and marginalized
individuals and families.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records in accordance with Section 181 (c) of GAAM, Volume I, and (b) henceforth, reconcile their records at least quarterly, so that errors or discrepancies that may be noted are adjusted accordingly.
2. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or the person responsible: i. Immediately transmit to the Municipal Accountant the identified unrecorded disbursements and receipts; ii. Make representations with the depository bank to obtain copies of the unrecorded debit and credit memos to facilitate recording thereof in the books of accounts; and iii. Ensure that the reports of disbursements and deposits and all its supporting documents are strictly monitored and completely transmitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.
3. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all the cash collections intact and regularly with the authorized government depository bank in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. The practice of retaining part of the collections should be discontinued.
4. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal Treasurer to settle his accountabilities amounting to ₱11,471,451.51 pertaining to undeposited collections during his term.
5. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the relevant and material information regarding the respective accountabilities of the former Municipal Treasurer and the incumbent Municipal Treasurer regarding the Cash – Local Treasury account balance as of year-end. Henceforth, the disclosure in the financial statements must be in accordance with IPSAS No. 1.
6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the closure of FCB Account No. 005-0106-00001-6 and transfer of current ending balance thereof to bank account of the Municipality with AGDB, or submit to the Audit Team the written justification if the Municipality intends to maintain the said FCB Account.
observations.managementAction: Refusal of the Municipal Treasurer or Former Municipal person responsible: The Audit Team issued 21 Notices of Suspension for the unrecorded disbursements. Demands were made to the former Municipal Treasurer. The Cash – Local Treasurer local balance of ₱2,117,048.47 was deposited on the first banking day of January 2019. All supporting documents of collections and disbursements are religiously transmitted to the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Treasurer requested for the Sangguniang Bayan to close the FCB account. Accountabilities of the former Municipal Treasurer was disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements of year 2019.
observations.reason: Refer to Audit Observation No. 1, Part II hereof.

No. 1445 on the proper grant and liquidation of cash advances; b. Ensure that officers and employees who will retire, resign or transfer to other agency are required to settle first their accountabilities before issuing the clearance from money and property accountability; and c. Make other appropriate actions such as, but not limited to, withholding of salaries of employee still employed by the Municipality, as provided under Paragraph 2 of Section 5.1.3, and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and filing of civil action without prejudice to the sanctions as provided under Section 128 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters are regularly served. The Management served. The practice of “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy was implemented and salary deductions were implemented for those who are still employed. The Municipal Accountant will monitor and ensure compliance.
observations.reason: There are still unliquidated accounts. Refer to Audit Observation No. 2, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: i. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of all its PPE, Investment Property and Biological Assets to determine the actual existence and condition of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. The Inventory Committee may coordinate and request the assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all properties under their respective offices and submit the inventory report to the committee for validation. Henceforth, conduct the physical count of PPE annually, and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of each year. ii. Instruct the Municipal GSO to reconstruct, update and maintain the required property cards; and iii. Instruct the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to cause the reconciliation of results of the actual count with the property and accounting records to ascertain the completeness and valuation of PPE, Investment Property and Biological Assets accounts. All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled immediately.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and Municipal Engineer conducted the physical count. The Municipal Accountant and GSO have initially harmonized their records by using property tags and assigning common property numbers to facilitate specific identification and reconciliation. However, only partial physical inventory was completed as reported.
observations.reason: Only 3.96% of the PPE were physically counted by the GSO and Municipal Engineer. Refer to Audit Observation No. 4, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: i. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation of all the programs, projects, activities charged under the 20% Development Fund to achieve effectively and efficiently the socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality; ii. Review the schedule of implementation, reflect the feasible target completion date in the Annual Investment Program, Priority Development Program, and Supplemental Investment Program, and faithfully comply with the targeted completion date; iii. Maximize the engagement through straight contract, that is, to enter into bid with one contractor for both the labor and materials for the infrastructure project; and iv. Augment the manpower resources of the Municipal Engineering Office by the HRMO filling-up the vacancy and/or by causing the approval of additional plantilla positions to do the job, subject to availability of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor called the attention of the MPDC and Municipal Engineer to implement efficiently and monitor all PPAs under the 20% Development Fund. Through the Municipal Development Council, 22 dormant PPAs were reprogrammed to four new PPAs. The Municipal Mayor directed the HRMO to immediately fill up the vacant proposed position for additional budget for that position.
observations.reason: Dormant PPAs were reprogrammed to four new PPAs; implementation still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: i. Coordinate with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to come up with a study or plan to attain the required purpose of having daily and intermediate soil cover of waste under Section 41(e) of R.A. No. 9003; and ii. Ensure the daily and intermediate soil cover of the waste in the sanitary landfill to avoid hazardous effects.
observations.managementAction: Soil covering of the landfill was done regularly. The Municipal purchased a backhoe for that purpose; however the backhoe was borrowed/detained in another project and not always available for the sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: The procured backhoe was not stationed in the Sanitary Landfill. This audit finding was included in Audit Observation No. 15, Part II hereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the School Board, require the preparation and submission of the CDP as prescribed under DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 indicating the strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of the SEF.
observations.managementAction: The School Board will strictly comply with the recommendation and DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Treasurer, exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the Special Education Fund to generate funds for the implementation of priority programs and projects for the benefit of public school children.
observations.managementAction: Delinquent real properties were auctioned by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office on November 12, 2019.

observations.managementAction: The BAC will require the submission of the necessary eligibility requirements of the suppliers starting year 2019 and onward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through its BAC, send RFQs to at least three (3) qualified suppliers (or contractors) in resorting to Shopping or Small Value Procurement as alternative method of procurement to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The BAC will send RFQs to at least three (3) qualified suppliers (or contractors) in resorting to Shopping or Small Value Procurement as alternative method of procurement starting year 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant (a) reconcile their records in accordance with Section 181 (c) of GAAM,
Volume I, and (b) henceforth, reconcile their records at least quarterly, so that errors
or discrepancies that may be noted are adjusted accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer or person responsible:
i. Immediately transmit to the Municipal Accountant the identified
unrecorded disbursements and receipts;
ii. Make representations with the depository bank to obtain copies of the
unrecorded debit and credit memos to facilitate recording thereof in
the books of accounts; and
iii. Ensure that the reports of disbursements and deposits and all its
supporting documents are strictly monitored and completely
transmitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer
to deposit all the cash collections intact and regularly with the authorized government
depository bank in compliance with Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I. The practice of retaining part of the collections should be discontinued.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the former Municipal
Treasurer to settle his accountabilities amounting to ₱11,471,451.51 pertaining to
undeposited collections during his term.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose in the Notes to
Financial Statements the relevant and material information regarding the respective
accountabilities of the former Municipal Treasurer and the incumbent Municipal
Treasurer regarding the Cash – Local Treasury account balance as of year-end.
Henceforth, the disclosure in the financial statements must be in accordance with
PPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the closure of FCB
Account No. 005-0106-00001-6 and transfer of current ending balance thereof to
bank account of the Municipality with AGDB, or submit to the Audit Team the
written justification if the Municipality intends to maintain the said FCB Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
i. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) send tracer/follow up letters
relative to the Demand Letters sent to the Accountable Officers enjoining
them the immediate settlement of their cash advances with due recourse to
the consequences that may be instituted against them to enforce liquidation;
(b) monitor strictly the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that
no additional cash advance is granted unless the previous ones are settled;
and (c) observe strictly the provision of COA Circular No. 97-002 and
Section 89 of P.D. No. 1445 on the proper grant and liquidation of cash
advances;
ii. Ensure that officers and employees who will retire, resign or transfer to
other agency are required to settle first their accountabilities before issuing
the clearance from money and property accountability; and
iii. Make other appropriate actions such as, but not limited to, withholding of
salaries of employee still employed by the Municipality, as provided under
Paragraph 2 of Section 5.1.3, and 5.9 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and
filing of civil action without prejudice to the sanctions as provided under Section
128 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
i. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an immediate physical count of
all its PPE, Investment Property and Biological Assets to determine the actual
existence and condition of the same, pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on
NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1. The Inventory Committee may coordinate and
request the assistance of the department heads to conduct inventory of all
properties under their respective offices and submit the inventory report to

annually, and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the Audit

ii. Instruct the Municipal GSO to reconstruct, update and maintain the required
property cards; and
iii. Instruct the Municipal GSO and the Municipal Accountant to cause the

records to ascertain the completeness and valuation of PPE, Investment
Property and Biological Assets accounts. All discrepancies between physical
and book inventories must be investigated, adjusted and reconciled
immediately.

i. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the

projects and activities charged under the 20% Development Fund to achieve

project; and
iv. Augment the manpower resources of the Municipal Engineering Office by
filling-up the vacancy and/or by causing the approval of additional plantilla
positions to do the job, subject to availability of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
i. Procure equipment, materials and supplies, install basic utilities such as
water and electricity, and designate personnel to facilitate the opening and
the operation of the new Slaughterhouse; and
ii. Refer the matter to Sangguniang Bayan for appropriate appropriation, if
necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
i. Coordinate with the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer
(MENRO) and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator
(MPDC) to come up with a study or plan to attain the required purpose of

We recommended that the Municipality faithfully comply with the
preparation and submission of the CDP as prescribed under DEPED-DBM-DILG
Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 indicating the strategic prioritization policies in the
allocation of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Treasurer,
exert additional effort in the collection of additional levy on real property for the
Special Education Fund to generate funds for the implementation of priority
programs and projects for the benefit of public school children.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC, require the
bidders/suppliers to submit the Mayor’s Permit, and PhilGEPS Registration
Number, if applicable, when alternative method of procurement is used.

We recommended that the Municipality, through its BAC, submit a
satisfactory explanation why quotations of prices were not obtained from qualified
suppliers. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC send RFQs to at least three (3)
qualified suppliers (or contractors) in resorting to Shopping or Small Value
Procurement as alternative method of procurement to ensure that the most

We reiterated our recommendation in the CY 2016 Annual Audit Report that the Assets Inventory Committee should fast track the completion of the reconciliation of the physical inventory count and reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following remedial measures to regulate the granting of financial assistance: • To complement the existing Administrative Order through enactment of Sangguniang Bayan ordinance/resolution. Said guidelines should include and contain clear-cut and more detailed policies and guidelines on the grant of donation/financial assistance. These guidelines should include and provide for the following, but not limited to: o the amount of donation/assistance to be given for each type of grant and in particular case, including the frequency or maximum number of entitlements in a year to each individual/family for an equitable delivery of services and resources to poor, marginalized and vulnerable/disadvantaged individuals; o approval limits for specific cases; o since all cases are referred to the MSWDO for eligibility evaluation, a case study report prepared by the MSWDO should be made part of the supporting documents of the claim. • Moreover, refrain from paying transactions not validly and completely documented. Otherwise, all payments for transactions not supported with valid and complete documentation shall be suspended/disallowed in audit and the officials and employees found to be directly responsible therefor shall be held personally liable pursuant to the 2009 Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction:
SB Resolution No. 2018-108B was enacted on June 25, 2018 to prescribe the guidelines for the utilization of the assistance to individuals in crisis situation.

The management will be more cautious in the review of the supporting documents for financial assistance and will ensure that only those claims that have valid and complete documentations will be paid.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Assets Inventory Committee
should fast track the completion of the reconciliation of the physical inventory count
and accounting records, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report. The
Municipal Mayor should assign additional manpower to the General Services
Officer’s Office to expedite the reconciliation process, undergo the actual physical
count, maintain inventory cards for each item of PPE, prepare the required annual
physical inventory report and monitor the same.

We recommended the GSO to maintain property cards and establish a
database of all PPEs of the Municipality to facilitate the reconciliation of the same to
the physical inventory count upon its completion.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management immediately take
the necessary actions to transfer the titles of those lands that are still under the name
of its previous owners in the name of the Municipality of Brooke’s Point.

We recommended that the management exert efforts to recognize the above
parcel of land as part of their PPE and to adhere to the requirements of PPSAS No.
17 in order to present complete and fair financial statements. The Municipal Assessor
to conduct the appraisal to facilitate the recording of the assets in the books of the
municipality and assert its claim of ownership thereon.

We recommended that the former Municipal Treasurer should submit his
cashbook during his previous term as required in Section 181(e) of GAAM, Volume
I, and Revised Penal Code. The Municipal Accountant should conduct reconciliation
between the GL and cashbook balances which should be observed at least quarterly
to avoid accumulation of unaccounted/unidentified discrepancies.

We recommended the Management to require its Accountable Officers to
liquidate the cash advances, through submission of the necessary supporting
documents and refund any excess amount, as the case may be.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to send demand letters to the
officials and employees with unliquidated accounts and cause the withholding of their
respective salaries, and see to it that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end
of each year. The Management shall strictly monitor cash advances to prevent
granting of additional cash advances to any official or employee with
unliquidated/outstanding balances.

Likewise, we recommended the Management and the Municipal Accountant to
properly maintain the books and supporting schedules with complete details of all
cash advances and require also strict adherence thereto.

Further, we recommended the Management to strictly comply with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 89 of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and BAC to comply with
COMELEC Resolutions and GPPB Circulars. We further recommended that rules
and regulations must always be complied with so that the interest of the public must
always prevail and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended the Management to insure all government properties with
the Property Insurance Fund of the GSIS.

We recommended the Municipal Engineer to conduct an inspection on the
mentioned projects, prepare the necessary report and submit to the Municipal
Accountant. The Municipal Accountant to adjust the books and transfer the PPE to
the Registry of Public Infrastructures (RPI).

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the Office of the
Municipal Accountant should conduct reconciliation of their accounts to
determine those projects that are on-going and completed and provide an updated
report of the same.

We further recommended that the municipality impose strict compliance with
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, and always provide the Audit Team
with updated information quarterly as to the status of the publicity of
projects/programs/activities in order to allow timely validation of the same.

We recommended the Management to stop the practice of charging expenses
using the LDRRMF appropriation. The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Council (LDRRMC) should rectify/amend the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Investment Plan (LDRRMIP) and include therein a
provision that all disbursements made against the LDRRMF in violation of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 in CY 2016 be returned/refunded by
the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at least
1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with the above provisions of RA 9344
and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 in order to ensure implementation
of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the overstatement of MOOE for CY 2016. The Municipal
Treasurer should prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant a list/schedule of
unclaimed/ unreleased checks as of December 31 for proper recognition and
recording in the books

We recommended that Management strictly enforce the provisions of the
General Appropriations Act (GAA) to ensure that each employee’s monthly net take-
home pay shall not be less than the minimum amount prescribed for the fiscal year
and that payroll deductions shall be limited following the order of preference stated

We recommended for the Municipal Mayor to require the concerned incumbent and former OIC Municipal Treasurers to transmit to the Municipal Accountant the check disbursement vouchers and supporting documents pertaining to the unaccounted withdrawals/ disbursements totalling ₱5,679,964.38. When the documents are submitted, the Municipal Accountant should facilitate the recording thereof in the books of accounts provided they are valid and proper disbursements/transactions. If warranted, administrative disciplinary action may be instituted against erring officials as provided under Section 127 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445. Further, these resulted in the overstatement of the Cash in Bank accounts and the understatement of the beginning balances amounting to ₱4,125,844.56 under the General Fund and ₱1,489.09 for SEF. The Municipality should also secure the pertinent cleared checks for the amount of ₱5,679,964.38 and submit authenticated copies thereof to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We therefore recommended for the Municipal Mayor to continue strictly enforcing the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-002 and COA Circular No. 2009-002. For officers and employees who are still in the service, require the immediate liquidation or collection either in cash or thru salary deduction. We likewise reiterated that clearance for money and property accountability should not be issued to officials and employees who are due for transfer/separation/retirement unless they have first settled their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that as of December 31, 2016, it had been reduced to P22,629,548.49 as a result of liquidation of any outstanding cash advances and salary deduction.
observations.reason: Implemented as a result of collection either in cash or thru salary deduction.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Management to submit authenticated copies of the Sangguniang Bayan Resolution/Ordinance ratifying the aforecited modifications or amendments to the SPLA as well as the duly stamped received notification and approval by the MDFO of such modifications. Otherwise, the requested implementation of such modifications shall constitute violation of the SPLA.
observations.managementAction: The management furnished the Audit Team of the documents being requested on March 10, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to submit the authority from the Office of the President to warrant the undertaking of the project by administration, to submit the required documents in compliance with Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to submit the Program of Work/Detailed Estimates corresponding to the ABC of the Subproject. Other noted deficiencies or deviations from RA 9184 and the Agency-to-Agency Agreement found to be irregular/illegal as defined in COA Circular No. 2012-003 shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management executed the project via the alternative method of procurement of negotiated procurement of procurement materials as one of its responsibilities and undertook the procurement of infrastructure projects, consulting services, and goods from another agency as sanctioned under Section 53.5 of the Implementing Agreement. The management would like to humbly submit that the general conditions mentioned in the Implementing Guidelines of the Agency-to-Agency Agreements were sufficiently complied with. They also acknowledged certain errata as to the signing of two complete sets of Agency-to-Agency Agreements and agreed to execute an addendum to contain the agreed Section V-B therein.
observations.reason: The management furnished the Audit Team the documents being requested on March 10, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended compliance with Article 61 of the IRR of RA No. 7160 on the conduct of public hearing by the Sangguniang Bayan. If public hearing was conducted, submit to the audit team the pertinent journal of proceedings. We recommended for the Municipality to coordinate with the Provincial Government for the execution and completion of the two (2) alliance projects, provided that the required public hearing/s was conducted. Otherwise, no subsequent transfer of municipal counterpart fund shall be made to PGP for the remaining alliance projects. Likewise, secure from the PGP the Statement of Receipts and Expenditures (SRE) for the fund transfer made as of December 31, 2015. We recommended the inclusion of penal provisions or sanctions in the MOA in case of non-compliance with the project implementation schedule subject to the ratification of the Sangguniang Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan. We recommended for the Municipality to ensure that the municipal counterpart fund shall be deposited in a joint bank account in accordance with Section 3.01 of MOA. We recommended the submission of authenticated copies of Schedules No. 1-6 forming part of the MOA including authenticated copies of resolutions passed by the Sangguniang Bayan and the Sangguniang Panlalawigan ratifying the duly signed MOA. We recommended that all modifications or amendments to the MOA, including changes in project cost, shall require ratification by the Sangguniang Bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan. We recommended the inclusion in the MOA of provision on ownership of alliance projects for proper recording in the books. We recommended that no fund shall be released to PGP for infrastructure projects unless supported with feasibility studies and complete detailed engineering outputs/documents mentioned in Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: As of March 10, 2017, the Provincial Government of Palawan has refunded to the Municipality the amount transferred to them for the alliance projects that had not been implemented. The Municipality initially prepared a check payable to DILG, the granting agency, to refund the unused balance. However, the DILG refused to receive it and directed them to refund it instead to the Bureau of Treasury (BoT). When they remitted it to the BoT, they likewise refused it.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the fund used for administrative expenses of LDRRMO which should have been appropriately charged against the General Fund totaling ₱623,100.63 be refunded/returned accordingly to the DRRM Fund (DRRMF). The MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer should henceforth strictly comply with the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 on the charges and utilization of DRRM fund.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer have already appropriated for various expenses under the General Fund effective budget year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to issue demand letters to delinquent taxpayers for the settlement of their taxes. List of delinquent taxpayers shall likewise be published. If demand letters were ignored, the Municipal Treasurer shall elevate the matter to the provincial level for the enforcement of the following provisions of the Local Government Code of 1991 to ensure the collection of delinquent real property taxes: a. Section 258. Levy on Real Property b. Section 260. Advertisement and Sale c. Section 266. Collection of Real Property Tax Through the Courts
observations.managementAction: A list of all RPT tax delinquencies was submitted to SB, copy furnished COA together with AAPSI 2014.
observations.reason: Had the Municipality intensified tax collection efforts, these amounts could have been used to sustain various operational needs, programs, projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: 1. Entitlement to mobile/ telephone expense shall be determined by the rank of the official/employee and phone line subscription shall be limited to one (1) unit for each entitled official as stated in Item No. 5, Annex D of COA Circular No. 2012-003. Payment found to be excessive shall be disallowed in audit. 2. Procurement of telephone/communications services for telephone/communication services shall be made in accordance with the provisions in Appendix 13 of the IRR of RA 9184. 3. Ensure that all claims for telephone/communication services under MOOE be supported with complete documents as required in Section 6.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Moreover, the Management is reminded to ensure that monthly allocation for Telephone Expenses should be at a rate not exceeding what is usual or proper, not unreasonably high and beyond just measure or amount and not in excess of reasonable limits, taking into account the prevailing rates or available promos offered by various telecommunication services providers. Likewise, submit a copy of existing memorandum/ order authorizing the claim of Telephone Expenses and its corresponding rate per entitled official/employee.
observations.managementAction: A memo was issued on October 10, 2016 to authorize the claim of telephone expenses and the corresponding rate for each entitled official/employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the unused fund balance be returned, the aforesaid warranty security be required from the Contractor, the as-built plan and clearance be submitted and that explanation be rendered for other noted deficiencies. Moreover, the grant, utilization and liquidation of fund transfers should be made in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 and that procurement process should comply with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality initially prepared a check payable to DILG, the granting agency, to refund the unused balance. However, the DILG refused to receive it and directed them to refund it instead to the Bureau of Treasury (BoT). When they remitted it to the BoT, they likewise refused it.
